item.title: Spotify Plans to Add Live Concert Video, Tickets
item.description: Spotify is looking to become a hub for live music

item.title: Korean Stocks Tumble as Investors Rush to Offload Tech Shares
item.description: South Korean stocks dropped, led by steep losses in chipmaker shares on an intensifying rotation out of artificial intelligence beneficiaries.

item.title: 8.1 Magnitude Quake Strikes Philippines, Tsunami Waves Possible
item.description: An earthquake of 8.1 magnitude struck the southern Philippine island of Mindanao on Monday, triggering a tsunami warning, damaging buildings and prompting evacuations on the first day of school.

item.title: Japan’s Growth Holds Up Despite Drop in Business Investment
item.description: Japan’s economy grew at a still solid pace at the start of the year even after the turbulence in Iran prompted businesses to cut investment.

item.title: Sheinbaum Unveils Affordable EV Prototype Backed by Government
item.description: An electric vehicle startup backed by the Mexican government unveiled its first prototype in a ceremony featuring President Claudia Sheinbaum, who praised the project as an affordable mobility option and a showcase for homegrown engineering.

item.title: Venezuela Posts Lowest Monthly Inflation Since 2024 in May
item.description: Venezuela’s central bank said inflation is set for a slowdown after May data showed the rise in the consumer price index declined to 6.3%, the lowest since October 2024.

item.title: Nvidia, SK Hynix Seal Multi-Year Pact to Develop AI Chips
item.description: Nvidia Corp. and SK Hynix Inc. have agreed to partner on designing future generations of memory chips for AI, a win for a South Korean leader vying with Samsung Electronics Co. in a red-hot arena.

item.title: Air New Zealand Steps Up Flight Cuts as Fuel Costs Mount
item.description: Air New Zealand will cut more flights in the three months through October as it faces rising jet fuel costs and soft demand, particularly in its home market, Chief Executive Officer Nikhil Ravishankar said.

item.title: Naver to Use Nvidia’s AI Models in Bid to Cement Lead in Korea
item.description: South Korea’s Naver Corp. agreed to build data centers based on Nvidia Corp.’s models in a move to clinch its dominance in the country’s artificial intelligence landscape.

item.title: Gold Holds Decline as Iran Attacks Threatens Mideast Ceasefire
item.description: Gold held a loss after Iran fired several rounds of missiles toward Israel, jeopardizing efforts to end the Middle East war that’s upended global markets.

item.title: Market Rout Puts Pressure on Indonesia to Deliver Concrete Steps
item.description: Indonesian authorities need to provide firmer policy guidance and unveil concrete steps to improve sentiment after last week’s selloff that pummeled stocks and the currency, according to analysts, who say investors will remain unconvinced by assurances alone.

item.title: Japan’s Stocks Set to Sink on Tech Selloff, Middle East Worries
item.description: Japanese stocks are set to fall, led by tech shares, after strong US jobs data fueled expectations that the Federal Reserve will keep rates higher for longer, sparking a selloff in AI-related shares on Wall Street.

item.title: US Stock Futures Drop After Tech Selloff, Oil Up: Markets Wrap
item.description: Equities looked set for more losses after a tech-led selloff on Wall Street Friday and a robust jobs report fueled expectations for an interest-rate hike by the Federal Reserve.

item.title: Oil Jumps as Iran’s Attacks on Israel Put Ceasefire at Risk
item.description: Oil rebounded after Iran fired several rounds of missiles toward Israel, threatening a fragile ceasefire as talks to end the war falter.

item.title: Peru on Edge as Exit Polls Show No Clear Presidential Winner
item.description: Peruvians were on edge Sunday as exit polls offered little clarity on which candidate would win the bitterly contested runoff to become the country’s next president.

item.title: Starmer to Roll Out UK Job Center Tools to Beat AI Work Threat
item.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer will promise to prevent any community from being left behind in the “tech revolution” by using artificial intelligence tools to help people find work.

item.title: Corporate Japan Borrows More as Deals, Outflows Pressure Ratings
item.description: Japanese businesses are ramping up borrowing to cover cash shortfalls fueled by record merger activity, booming capital investments and mounting investor pressure for shareholder returns, putting their credit ratings at risk.

item.title: Bond Traders Bet on a CPI Surge That Bolsters Case for Fed Pivot
item.description: Bond traders are wagering that inflation figures this week will show the strongest price pressures in several years, adding to pressure on the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates.

item.title: Goldman Sachs No Longer Expects Fed Interest-Rate Cut This Year
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. economists no longer expect the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates this year due to a stronger-than-expected labor market.

item.title: Kevin O’Leary’s Huge Data Center in Canada Faces a Skeptical Public
item.description: A proposal by a Kevin O’Leary-backed firm to build Canada’s largest data center in northwestern Alberta is facing pushback from some residents and Indigenous groups.

item.title: Brazil Budget Freeze Grounds Aircraft Approval Process
item.description: Brazil’s decision to freeze part of the budget of its civil aviation regulator is threatening to delay the arrival of new aircraft and planned capacity expansions just as airlines race to meet robust travel demand and address fleet shortages.

item.title: Ingredion Is in Advanced Talks to Acquire Tate & Lyle for £2.7 billion
item.description: Ingredion Inc. is in advanced talks to acquire Tate & Lyle Plc in a deal valued at £2.7 billion ($3.6 billion), people with knowledge of the matter said, as another famous brand prepares to leaves the London stock market.

item.title: Inside Apple’s Secret Meeting That Led It to Finally Take AI Seriously
item.description: Also: What to expect at WWDC 2026.

item.title: Worries Grow That Hedge Fund Crowding May Amplify Risk in Crisis
item.description: Friday’s sudden equities rout after a months-long rally is renewing concerns that the unwinding of crowded trades could exacerbate market losses.

item.title: GE Sees Potential For More China Deals After Trump-Xi Meeting
item.description: GE Aerospace remains optimistic that it can secure more aircraft-engine orders from China after President Donald Trump and his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping met in Beijing last month and manufacturing partner Boeing Co. came home an initial batch of commitments.

item.title: Trump Says Fed Rate Increase Would Be Wrong, Again Calls for Cut
item.description: President Donald Trump said Federal Reserve policymakers would be wrong to raise interest rates after a blowout US jobs report, while insisting he doesn’t want to influence Kevin Warsh before he chairs his first Fed meeting.

item.title: Nvidia’s CEO Says New Vera Chip Will Use SK Hynix’s Memory Chips
item.description: Nvidia Corp. Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang said its new Vera central processing units will use SK Hynix Inc.’s memory chips as the two companies prepare to do more business in the coming year.

item.title: AI’s Mega Stock Deals Raise Specter of More Shares Than Buyers
item.description: A flood of new shares from companies looking to fund their artificial intelligence ambitions is raising questions on Wall Street about whether there will be enough buyers to soak them all up and what this pile of fresh equity will mean for stock prices more broadly.

item.title: OPEC+ Agrees Another Symbolic Quota Hike for July
item.description: Major OPEC+ members agreed another modest symbolic increase to their oil output quotas for July, even as a blockage of exports from the Persian Gulf prevents most of them from implementing it.

item.title: EM Long Bonds Seen Missing Out on Any Iran Peace Dividend
item.description: Emerging-market bond investors expecting a broad rally from any US-Iran peace deal may be in for a disappointment, as money managers see sticky inflation and fiscal concerns keeping long-term yields elevated.
